derived.causal_summary: Phase 009 is the final cutover-and-rollout. Investigation revealed the sk-code-review removal is not a bounded alias delete but a retune of ~100 calibrated entries across the production advisor scorer (TS + Python mirror) plus a graph regeneration and coupled-corpora rebaseline — and that the load-bearing steps cannot run in the worktree (the graph compiler fails validation on another skill's un-built dist paths; the TS scorer can't load @spec-kit/shared). The phase therefore split at the verifiability seam. The BRANCH portion landed and verified: 8 harness-test migrations to the hub layout (suite 8-failed to 1-failed, the 1 being a sk-design-owned stale digest), the parent-hub-vocab-sync generalization (registry-derived mode prefixes; sk-design unchanged; sk-code orphans 47->13), the CS-003 word-boundary matcher fix (preview no longer false-matches review), a 29-path content-drift fix in smart_routing.md (0 dead paths, also lifting the 008 benchmark), and two fold-broken live fixes (setup-cp-sandbox.sh, a dead README link). The MAIN-SIDE ROLLOUT is specified as one atomic advisor-rebuild runbook (build dist -> remove scorer identity TS+Python -> rebaseline corpora -> regen graph node 19->18 -> reindex -> delete 4 alias sites -> repoint ~350 NAME refs -> cut version release -> full verification), sequenced so no step exposes an unresolvable identity, with the sk-code-review alias kept as a working shim until the rebuild lands it all at once.
